title: Bit Manipulation — The Art of the Mask
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Bit manipulation macros (set, clear, toggle, test) compile to single instructions on any reasonable compiler
content.proof: #define SET_BIT(x, b) ((x) |= (1U << (b))) compiles to ORRS instruction on ARM. #define CLEAR_BIT(x, b) ((x) &= ~(1U << (b))) compiles to BICS. No function call, no branch, no overhead. The preprocessor runs before the optimizer even sees the code.
content.benchmark: Macro bit operations: 1 cycle each. Inline function equivalent: 1 cycle (compiler inlines). Library function: 15+ cycles (function call overhead + parameter passing).
content.caveat: Double-evaluation hazard in macros. Always parenthesize macro parameters. Or use inline functions in C99 and let the compiler do the right thing.
